## OpenAPI operation `summary` (the payment-prompt reason)

When a `pay` client hits a paid endpoint, the user sees a system-level biometric prompt — TouchID on macOS, Hello on Windows — with three lines:

```
authorize a payment of $0.001 from ludo.

reason:   <-- this comes from your OpenAPI operation
operator: bigquery.google.gateway-402.com.
```

The **`reason:`** line is the only sentence between a user and approving a debit. A vague summary defeats the consent UX; a misleading one is a phishing surface. Write it like it's the only thing the user reads — because it is.

### Where the text comes from

For each operation in your committed `openapi.json`, `pay` resolves the prompt reason in this order:

1. `operation.summary` — **preferred**, this is what you should write.
2. `operation.description` — fallback used only when `summary` is missing.
3. `"{METHOD} {path}"` — last-resort placeholder; never display-clean.

Reach for `summary`. Leave `description` for the longer prose that documents the operation for developers.

> Keep it ≤ **60 characters**. The prompt truncates at 64 — anything longer ends in `…`.

### Do

- Lead with a concrete verb: `Run`, `Search`, `Solve`, `Generate`, `Translate`, `Send`, `Create`, `Fetch`, `Submit`.
- Name the domain object: `Run a BigQuery SQL query`, not `Run a query`.
- Stay between 16 and 60 characters.
- Match what *this specific call* does. A batch endpoint says "batch"; a single-shot endpoint doesn't.
- Use ASCII letters, digits, spaces, and basic punctuation only.
- Give every operation in the spec its own distinct summary.

### Don't

- ❌ Empty / whitespace-only `summary` (and no `description` fallback).
- ❌ Generic placeholders: `"API access"`, `"Endpoint"`, `"Request"`, `"Call"`.
- ❌ A verbatim path echo: `"GET /v1/foo"`, `"POST /search"`.
- ❌ Marketing copy: `"Fastest", "Best-in-class", "Premium", "Free!"`.
- ❌ Unresolved templates: `"{{operationId}}"`, `"<TODO>"`, `"FIXME"`, `"TBD"`.
- ❌ Under-stating side effects or cost: `"Free quick lookup"` on a paid endpoint, `"Read X"` when the call mutates state.
- ❌ Emoji, markdown link syntax `[…](…)`, smart quotes — they may render oddly in the OS prompt.
- ❌ The same `summary` reused across multiple operations.

### Examples

| ✅ Good | ❌ Bad | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `Run a synchronous BigQuery SQL query` | `Run query` | Names provider + shape |
| `Search places near a coordinate` | `Endpoint` | Concrete verb + object |
| `Solve a reCAPTCHA v2 challenge` | `Solve captcha` | Specific challenge type |
| `Generate an image from a text prompt` | `Image gen` | Full sentence, no jargon |
| `Translate text between two languages` | `API access` | Not the generic fallback |
| `Fetch a wallet's USDC balance on Solana` | `GET /v1/balance` | Reason, not path echo |
| `Send an SMS to a phone number` | `📱 SMS!` | No emoji, plain sentence |

### Validation contract (enforced by `pay catalog check`)

`pay catalog check` runs the rules below on every **priced** operation (operations with a `pricing` block in inline `endpoints:`, or operations the live probe classifies as 402-gated). Free operations are exempt — their summary never reaches a payment prompt.

|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | Has a `summary` or `description` | **error** | Both fields missing or whitespace-only |
| S2 | Effective text ≥ 12 chars | **error** | Too short to be informative |
| S3 | Effective text ≤ 80 chars | **error** | Will truncate in the prompt at 64 |
| S4 | Not a generic placeholder | **error** | Equals (case-insensitive) one of: `api access`, `endpoint`, `request`, `call`; or is exactly `"{METHOD} {path}"` |
| S5 | No unresolved template token | **error** | Contains `{{`, `<TODO>`, `FIXME`, `TBD`, `XXX`, `your-…-here` |
| S6 | No emoji / no markdown link syntax | **error** | Codepoint range U+1F000–U+1FFFF, or `](` substring |
| S7 | ASCII-printable | **error** | Contains characters outside `[\x20-\x7E]` (rejects smart quotes, RTL marks, zero-width) |
| S8 | Distinct across operations | **warning** | Same effective text reused by two or more operations in this spec |
| S9 | Starts with an action verb | **warning** | First token not in the verb allowlist (`Run`, `Search`, `Get`, `Fetch`, `List`, `Create`, `Submit`, `Send`, `Generate`, `Solve`, `Translate`, `Validate`, `Verify`, `Lookup`, `Resolve`, `Score`, `Detect`, `Classify`, `Extract`, `Parse`, …) |
| S10 | No marketing superlatives | **warning** | Contains `best`, `fastest`, `premium`, `cheapest`, `unlimited`, `free` (case-insensitive) as a whole word |

CLI surface (proposed; not yet implemented):

```text

pay catalog check <PAY.md> --no-check-summaries   # opt-out for legacy migration
pay catalog check <PAY.md> --summaries-only       # only run S1–S10
```

Until `pay catalog check` ships these rules, reviewers should hand-check the `summary` of every paid operation against the table above.

# pay-skills

A community registry of stablecoin-gated APIs for [pay](https://github.com/solana-foundation/pay) — an open directory the `pay` CLI and AI agents can search, route to, and pay through. Public good, community-maintained.

## Add your API in three commands

```bash
# 1. Scaffold a new provider — fetches the OpenAPI doc once, writes the spec
#    to providers/<your-org>/<api-name>/openapi.json, and creates a PAY.md
#    pre-filled with title, description, service_url, and TODO placeholders.
pay catalog scaffold <your-org>/<api-name> https://api.example.com/openapi.json

# 2. Edit the generated providers/<your-org>/<api-name>/PAY.md:
#      - replace the `category: TODO` placeholder
#      - replace the `use_case: TODO` placeholder
#    (TODO values fail validation, so you'll catch unfinished work locally.)
#    The committed openapi.json is what the catalog ships — review it.

# 3. Validate locally — parses frontmatter, resolves the committed OpenAPI,
#    probes every endpoint, applies the Solana-compat verdict.
pay catalog check providers/<your-org>/<api-name>/PAY.md
```

When `pay catalog check` prints `PAY.md check successful`, open a PR. CI runs the same pipeline; once merged, your API is live in `pay skills search` within minutes.

## Provider directory layout

```
providers/
  <operator>/<name>/PAY.md                 ← native API
  <operator>/<origin>/<name>/PAY.md        ← proxied API (gateway)
```

Each provider lives in its own directory. The directory's path under `providers/` becomes the provider FQN: `agentmail/email`, `merit-systems/stablecrypto/market-data`, `solana-foundation/google/translate`. You can co-locate sidecar files (e.g. `openapi.json`) next to `PAY.md` and reference them with `openapi: { path: openapi.json }` — the build inlines the resolved spec into the published index.

## PAY.md structure

```markdown
---
name: rpc                                  # must match parent directory name
title: "QuickNode"
description: "..."                         # 64–255 chars, powers search
use_case: "Use for ..."                    # 32–255 chars, helps LLMs route
category: compute                          # see categories list below
service_url: https://x402.quicknode.com    # production HTTPS URL, no IPs
openapi:
  path: openapi.json                       # spec must be committed in the PR
---

Free-form prose. Explain what the API offers, when an agent should reach
for it, and any spend-aware patterns (prefer narrow lookups over broad
searches, reuse identifiers, cap result limits, …).

## Spend-aware usage

- TODO: list patterns that minimize paid calls.
```

**Required frontmatter:** `name` `title` `description` `use_case` `category` `service_url` plus exactly one of `openapi:` or inline `endpoints:`.

**Categories:** `ai_ml` `cloud` `compute` `data` `devtools` `finance` `identity` `maps` `media` `messaging` `other` `productivity` `search` `security` `shopping` `storage` `translation`

**Optional:** `version`, `sandbox_service_url`.

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for the full field reference and writing guide.

## Local commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `pay catalog scaffold <fqn> <openapi_url>` | Generate a starter `<fqn>/PAY.md` from an OpenAPI document. |
| `pay catalog check <PAY.md>` | Validate one provider: frontmatter shape, OpenAPI resolution, live probe, Solana verdict. |
| `pay catalog check . --no-probe` | Walk the entire registry; frontmatter-only check (fast, offline-ish). |
| `pay catalog check . --changed-from origin/main` | Probe + verdict only on providers changed since `origin/main`. Mirrors PR CI. |
| `pay catalog build .` | Full registry build → writes `dist/skills.json` and per-provider detail JSONs. Used on `main` only. |

`-v` / `--verbose` on any `check` adds the per-endpoint probe + verdict tables. `--strict` upgrades non-Solana warnings to blocking errors.

## Security in one paragraph

Agents route real funds through this catalog, so the security boundary is the PR. **The OpenAPI spec must be committed to this repo** (`openapi: { path: openapi.json }` or `content:`). Remote `openapi: { url: ... }` is not accepted — it would let an operator pass review with one spec and serve a different one to agents after merge. Any change to a provider's API surface (new endpoints, new pricing, new schemas) goes through a new PR with the diff visible. See [Security & supply-chain policy](CONTRIBUTING.md#security--supply-chain-policy) for the full rules and reviewer checklist.

## Payment requirements

Paid endpoints must:

- Return HTTP **402** with a valid payment challenge (MPP or x402 protocol).
- Accept payment on **Solana mainnet**.
- Accept **USDC** or **USDT** stablecoins.

CI probes every endpoint and emits `::warning::` / `::error::` annotations inline on PRs. Endpoints that don't return a valid Solana 402 challenge block the merge. Indeterminate statuses (auth-required, SIWX, body-required) neither warn nor error — they pass through.

## How publication works

1. Contributors open PRs adding/editing PAY.md files (and optional sidecars). The `Validate providers` workflow parses, probes, and emits inline annotations.
2. On merge to `main`, the `Build & Publish Skills Index` workflow:
   - Re-runs the Solana-compat gate against this commit's diff (so a bypassed PR can't slip through).
   - Pulls the previous dist from `gs://pay-skills/v1/`.
   - Runs an incremental build (`--only <changed-fqns> --previous-dist prev-dist`) — unchanged providers are copied verbatim.
   - Publishes `dist/` to `gs://pay-skills/v1/`.
3. `pay skills update` clients refetch `index.json` and only the category buckets they need (one shard per category). The full per-provider detail is fetched on demand under `providers/<fqn>.json`. See [Published catalog layout](CONTRIBUTING.md#published-catalog-layout) for the on-disk shape.

Keep it ≤ 60 chars, verb-first, and specific. See [OpenAPI operation `summary` guidance](CONTRIBUTING.md#openapi-operation-summary-the-payment-prompt-reason) for do's, don'ts, and the validation rules CI enforces.

# pay-skills

A community registry of stablecoin-gated APIs for [pay](https://github.com/solana-foundation/pay) — an open directory the `pay` CLI and AI agents can search, route to, and pay through. Public good, community-maintained.

## Add your API in three commands

```bash
# 1. Scaffold a new provider — fetches the OpenAPI doc once, writes the spec
#    to providers/<your-org>/<api-name>/openapi.json, and creates a PAY.md
#    pre-filled with title, description, service_url, and TODO placeholders.
pay catalog scaffold <your-org>/<api-name> https://api.example.com/openapi.json

# 2. Edit the generated providers/<your-org>/<api-name>/PAY.md:
#      - replace the `category: TODO` placeholder
#      - replace the `use_case: TODO` placeholder
#    (TODO values fail validation, so you'll catch unfinished work locally.)
#    The committed openapi.json is what the catalog ships — review it.

# 3. Validate locally — parses frontmatter, resolves the committed OpenAPI,
#    probes every endpoint, applies the Solana-compat verdict.
pay catalog check providers/<your-org>/<api-name>/PAY.md
```

When `pay catalog check` prints `PAY.md check successful`, open a PR. CI runs the same pipeline; once merged, your API is live in `pay skills search` within minutes.

## Provider directory layout

```
providers/
  <operator>/<name>/PAY.md                 ← native API
  <operator>/<origin>/<name>/PAY.md        ← proxied API (gateway)
```

Each provider lives in its own directory. The directory's path under `providers/` becomes the provider FQN: `agentmail/email`, `merit-systems/stablecrypto/market-data`, `solana-foundation/google/translate`. You can co-locate sidecar files (e.g. `openapi.json`) next to `PAY.md` and reference them with `openapi: { path: openapi.json }` — the build inlines the resolved spec into the published index.

## PAY.md structure

```markdown
---
name: rpc                                  # must match parent directory name
title: "QuickNode"
description: "..."                         # 64–255 chars, powers search
use_case: "Use for ..."                    # 32–255 chars, helps LLMs route
category: compute                          # see categories list below
service_url: https://x402.quicknode.com    # production HTTPS URL, no IPs
openapi:
  path: openapi.json                       # spec must be committed in the PR
---

Free-form prose. Explain what the API offers, when an agent should reach
for it, and any spend-aware patterns (prefer narrow lookups over broad
searches, reuse identifiers, cap result limits, …).

## Spend-aware usage

- TODO: list patterns that minimize paid calls.
```

**Required frontmatter:** `name` `title` `description` `use_case` `category` `service_url` plus exactly one of `openapi:` or inline `endpoints:`.

**Operation summaries matter.** For every paid operation in your committed `openapi.json`, the `summary` field becomes the `reason:` line a user reads on the OS biometric prompt before approving a payment. Keep it ≤ 60 chars, verb-first, and specific. See [OpenAPI operation `summary` guidance](CONTRIBUTING.md#openapi-operation-summary-the-payment-prompt-reason) for do's, don'ts, and the validation rules CI enforces.

**Categories:** `ai_ml` `cloud` `compute` `data` `devtools` `finance` `identity` `maps` `media` `messaging` `other` `productivity` `search` `security` `shopping` `storage` `translation`

**Optional:** `version`, `sandbox_service_url`.

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for the full field reference and writing guide.

## Local commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `pay catalog scaffold <fqn> <openapi_url>` | Generate a starter `<fqn>/PAY.md` from an OpenAPI document. |
| `pay catalog check <PAY.md>` | Validate one provider: frontmatter shape, OpenAPI resolution, live probe, Solana verdict. |
| `pay catalog check . --no-probe` | Walk the entire registry; frontmatter-only check (fast, offline-ish). |
| `pay catalog check . --changed-from origin/main` | Probe + verdict only on providers changed since `origin/main`. Mirrors PR CI. |
| `pay catalog build .` | Full registry build → writes `dist/skills.json` and per-provider detail JSONs. Used on `main` only. |

`-v` / `--verbose` on any `check` adds the per-endpoint probe + verdict tables. `--strict` upgrades non-Solana warnings to blocking errors.

## Security in one paragraph

Agents route real funds through this catalog, so the security boundary is the PR. **The OpenAPI spec must be committed to this repo** (`openapi: { path: openapi.json }` or `content:`). Remote `openapi: { url: ... }` is not accepted — it would let an operator pass review with one spec and serve a different one to agents after merge. Any change to a provider's API surface (new endpoints, new pricing, new schemas) goes through a new PR with the diff visible. See [Security & supply-chain policy](CONTRIBUTING.md#security--supply-chain-policy) for the full rules and reviewer checklist.

## Payment requirements

Paid endpoints must:

- Return HTTP **402** with a valid payment challenge (MPP or x402 protocol).
- Accept payment on **Solana mainnet**.
- Accept **USDC** or **USDT** stablecoins.

CI probes every endpoint and emits `::warning::` / `::error::` annotations inline on PRs. Endpoints that don't return a valid Solana 402 challenge block the merge. Indeterminate statuses (auth-required, SIWX, body-required) neither warn nor error — they pass through.

## How publication works

1. Contributors open PRs adding/editing PAY.md files (and optional sidecars). The `Validate providers` workflow parses, probes, and emits inline annotations.
2. On merge to `main`, the `Build & Publish Skills Index` workflow:
   - Re-runs the Solana-compat gate against this commit's diff (so a bypassed PR can't slip through).
   - Pulls the previous dist from `gs://pay-skills/v1/`.
   - Runs an incremental build (`--only <changed-fqns> --previous-dist prev-dist`) — unchanged providers are copied verbatim.
   - Publishes `dist/` to `gs://pay-skills/v1/`.
3. `pay skills update` clients refetch `index.json` and only the category buckets they need (one shard per category). The full per-provider detail is fetched on demand under `providers/<fqn>.json`. See [Published catalog layout](CONTRIBUTING.md#published-catalog-layout) for the on-disk shape.

1	//! Resolve a [`OpenapiSource`] into a list of [`EndpointSpec`] entries.
2	//!
3	//! Provider specs can declare their endpoints in one of two mutually-exclusive
4	//! ways: an inline `endpoints:` list, or an `openapi:` source pointing to (or
5	//! inlining) an OpenAPI 3 document. When the latter is set the prober walks
6	//! `paths × methods` to synthesize the candidate endpoint list, which the
7	//! probe pipeline then hits to determine which are stablecoin-gated.
8	//!
9	//! Body generation: for POST/PUT/PATCH operations we also extract (or
10	//! synthesize) a request body so the probe doesn't get rejected with a 400
11	//! before reaching the paywall. Priority order:
12	//!   1. `requestBody.content."application/json".example`
13	//!   2. `requestBody.content."application/json".examples.<first>.value`
14	//!   3. `requestBody.content."application/json".schema.example`
15	//!   4. `requestBody.content."application/json".schema.examples[0]` (3.1)
16	//!   5. schema-derived dummy values (required fields only, `$ref`-resolved,
17	//!      format-aware: `email`, `uri`, `date-time`, `uuid`).
18	
19	use std::time::Duration;
20	
21	use pay_types::registry::{EndpointSpec, OpenapiSource, ProviderFrontmatter};
22	use reqwest::blocking::Client;
23	use serde_json::{Map, Value, json};
24	use tracing::debug;
25	
26	use crate::{Error, Result};
27	
28	const HTTP_METHODS: &[&str] = &["get", "post", "put", "patch", "delete"];
29	const FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 15;
30	const MAX_SCHEMA_DEPTH: u32 = 6;
31	
32	/// One endpoint resolved from an OpenAPI document — both the spec entry that
33	/// gets published to the index and the optional probe body extracted from
34	/// `requestBody`.
35	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
36	pub struct ResolvedEndpoint {
37	    pub spec: EndpointSpec,
38	    /// Serialized JSON body. `None` for GET/DELETE or when the OpenAPI doc
39	    /// does not declare a request body for the operation.
40	    pub body_example: Option<String>,
41	}
42	
43	/// Fetch / read the document referenced by `source` and synthesize an
44	/// endpoint list from it.
45	///
46	/// `service_url` is used to resolve [`OpenapiSource::Path`] (treated as a path
47	/// relative to the provider's `service_url`).
48	pub fn resolve_endpoints(
49	    source: &OpenapiSource,
50	    service_url: &str,
51	) -> Result<Vec<ResolvedEndpoint>> {
52	    let body = load_document(source, service_url, None)?;
53	    parse_endpoints(&body)
54	}
55	
56	/// Pure parser — no I/O. Synthesize endpoint specs from an OpenAPI JSON body.
57	///
58	/// Dispatches on the document's shape:
59	/// - **OpenAPI 3 / Swagger 2** (`openapi:` or `swagger:` key): walk
60	///   `paths.{path}.{method}`.
61	/// - **Google Discovery** (`kind: discovery#restDescription`): walk
62	///   `resources.*.methods.*` recursively (and any top-level `methods`).
63	///
64	/// Description is taken from the operation's `summary` first, then
